response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced polyglot engineer with genuine developer-tooling credentials — he worked on the LabWindows/CVI IDE at National Instruments and has authored test frameworks and language libraries — but the role's core requirements are absent. There is no evidence of Kotlin or Spring experience, no deep JVM work, and Java appears only peripherally (robotic arms, a device-orchestration tool) nearly a decade ago. His recent career centers on TypeScript/Node.js product work and DLang systems programming, not enterprise JVM ecosystems, making him a poor fit for a role demanding strong Java/Kotlin and Spring expertise from day one.
strengths: Direct IDE development experience (C/C++ components for NI LabWindows/CVI) and clear interest in language tooling
Author of open-source developer tools (test runner, assertion library, web server library), showing tooling and API-design instincts
Long track record across complex systems, including low-latency high-throughput services and platform architecture
concerns: No demonstrated Kotlin or Spring/enterprise-framework experience, which are hard requirements
Java exposure is minimal, dated (2016-2018), and not in enterprise or JVM-internals contexts
Recent focus is full-stack TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, far from the enterprise JVM tooling domain

key_factors.factor: Broad senior engineering experience on complex systems
